content: EHR impact assessment shows mixed results: Benefits - medication error reduction 50-80%, preventive service delivery improvement 10-20%, care coordination through information sharing, population health management capabilities, research data availability. Harms/limitations - clinician burnout (40-50% prevalence), alert fatigue leading to missed critical warnings, copy-paste errors propagating inaccurate information, cybersecurity vulnerabilities (healthcare has highest ransomware rates), digital divide limiting patient portal use. Cost-benefit analyses show $30,000-50,000 per physician in implementation costs with 3-5 year ROI through billing improvements, reduced transcription, and avoided duplicate testing. Interoperability gaps persist: only 30-40% of providers can electronically find/receive patient data from outside organizations.
